page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 86 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes dedicated Intel Xeon processors with 48 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The cache hierarchy includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and a 38 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, BMI2, and FMA. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. Single-core CPU performance is weak, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests. However, multi-core CPU performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core operations. Memory read bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top 25% for both cached and uncached read-only operations, though uncached write bandwidth is average. The instance demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations, reaching 63.93 million operations per second, and performs strongly in Passmark extended instructions. Conversely, LLM text generation speeds are weak, and single-threaded compression tasks are slow.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n1-highcpu-96 offers a high density of compute resources relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for highly parallelized workloads that do not require massive RAM allocations. The low memory-to-vCPU ratio of approximately 0.9 GB per core represents a tradeoff, rendering it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale in-memory processing. It is best suited for multi-threaded compute workloads, batch processing, software compilation, ray tracing, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance configured with 96 vCPUs and 86 GB of RAM. Powered by dedicated Intel Xeon processors with 48 physical cores, it features a 38 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512F instructions. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show weak single-core execution but strong multi-core CPU capabilities and high memory read bandwidth. It excels in parallel operations, such as Redis SET tasks, while exhibiting average performance in software compilation and ray tracing. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 0.9 GB per core, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for compute-dense, highly parallelized workloads. It is best suited for multi-threaded batch processing, web serving, and CPU-bound engineering simulations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 86 GB RAM, and Intel Xeon processors. It delivers strong multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-96 features 96 vCPUs and 86 GB RAM, offering strong multi-core performance for compute-heavy parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-core compute instance designed for parallelized workloads, offering high memory read bandwidth and dedicated Intel Xeon processing power.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 86 GB RAM powered by dedicated Intel Xeon processors
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory read bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Low memory-to-core ratio optimized for compute-heavy tasks
Well-suited for multi-threaded batch processing and web serving
